task: I need to send a short note to a subcontractor about a missed step on a job. What would be a clear way to word it so the message is easy to understand and can be used right away?

prefs.short: Wants blunt answers without hedging or fluff
prefs.explanation: He comes across as someone who values straight talk and dislikes being softened up. Given his hard-nosed, efficiency-first style, he is likely to prefer clear guidance over polite ambiguity. He probably sees excessive caution in wording as a waste of time.

prefs.short: Prefers concise responses that get to the point
prefs.explanation: His work style is all about no wasted motion, so he is likely to want answers that are brief and useful. Long explanations would probably feel like drag unless he specifically asks for them. He would rather get the key action than read a lot of setup.

prefs.short: Likes information laid out in simple bullets or checklists
prefs.explanation: Someone who manages jobsite logistics and paperwork would likely respond well to organized, scannable formats. Bullets and checklists fit his practical mindset because they make next steps obvious. He would probably dislike meandering prose when he needs to act quickly.

His persona suggests he does not want warmth or cheerfulness layered on top of the message. A steady, factual tone matches how he handles problems and authority. He would likely find overly friendly or emotionally expressive help distracting.

prefs.short: Can take firm pushback if it protects the job or avoids mistakes
prefs.explanation: He is used to being corrected by procedures, inspections, and hard realities, so he likely accepts pushback when it is justified. What matters to him is whether the objection is practical and protects outcomes, not whether it feels pleasant. He would probably reject soft disagreement that sounds indecisive or preachy.

turns.agent_response:
Here’s a clear, ready-to-send version:

**Subject:** Missed Step on Job

Hi [Subcontractor Name],  
We noticed that **[missed step]** was not completed on **[job name/address]**. Please review the scope for **Subcontract #[number]** and complete that item as soon as possible. If there is any issue or if the scope has changed, let me know right away so we can address it.  

Please confirm receipt and your plan to correct it.  

Thanks,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can also make it **firmer**, **more diplomatic**, or **more detailed for a specific trade**.

per_pref.reason: Uses direct wording like "We noticed that [missed step] was not completed" and "Please confirm receipt and your plan to correct it," with little hedging.
Keeps it fairly concise and immediately usable; the note is short and to the point, though it includes a small offer to customize further.
It is presented as a ready-to-send note with clear paragraph structure, but not in bullets or a checklist.
Tone is matter-of-fact and no-nonsense: "was not completed," "complete that item as soon as possible," and "let me know right away."
Includes firm pushback without being preachy: "If there is any issue or if the scope has changed, let me know right away so we can address it."

turns.agent_response:
That works. Here’s a tightened version you can send:

**Subject:** Missed Step on [Job Name]

[Name],  
You missed **[step]** at **[job/address]**. That needs to be corrected **before any further work on that area**.  
Review your scope and fix it now. If there’s an issue with the scope or site conditions, call me right away.

Confirm when it’s done.

[Your Name]

If you want, I can make it even more direct or turn it into a text-message version.

per_pref.reason: Very blunt and unsmoothed: "You missed [step]" and "That needs to be corrected" with no hedging.
Concise and to the point; gives a ready-to-send note with minimal extra explanation.
Uses a simple, scannable structure with a subject line and short paragraphs, though not bullets/checklist.
Matter-of-fact, no-nonsense tone throughout: "Review your scope and fix it now" and "Confirm when it’s done."
Includes firm pushback that protects the job: "That needs to be corrected before any further work" and "If there’s an issue... call me right away."
